pilot_config.purpose: Validate feasibility of full-model Hessian computation, attribution pipelines, and statistical analysis before committing to full-scale experiments

pilots.description: Run reanalysis script on existing Probe data. Verify GUM uncertainty budget decomposition and correlation matrix computation.
pilots.pass_criteria: Reanalysis script runs without error AND produces valid JSON with all statistics (class-conditional means, within-class variance, correlation matrix)
pilots.risk: Low - pure analysis on cached data
pilots.fallback: Debug script errors; simplify statistics if statsmodels issues

pilots.description: Set up environment, train ResNet-18 for 20 epochs (quick convergence check), then test full-model Hessian API on 10 test points. This is the CRITICAL pilot: if full-model Hessian fails (OOM or API limitation), the entire Phase 1+ design must be revised.
pilots.pass_criteria: Model trains to >70% test accuracy at 20 epochs AND full-model EK-FAC IF computes for 10 test points without OOM on single GPU AND K-FAC IF computes for same 10 points
pilots.risk: HIGH - full-model Hessian may OOM on ResNet-18. pyDVL/dattri may only support last-layer.
pilots.fallback: If full-model OOM: (1) try gradient checkpointing, (2) try last-2-layers only, (3) fall back to custom implementation using torch.autograd.functional.hessian on smaller batch. If API limitation: switch to dattri or custom Kronecker factorization.

pilots.description: Compute all 4 attribution methods (EK-FAC IF, K-FAC IF, RepSim, TRAK-50) for 100 test points (10/class). Verify output format, non-trivial variance in J10, and reasonable compute time extrapolation.
pilots.pass_criteria: All 4 methods produce valid rankings for 100 points AND Jaccard@10(EK-FAC, K-FAC) std > 0.05 AND per-point LDS has non-trivial spread AND total time < 15 min (extrapolates to <75min for 500 points)
pilots.risk: Medium - TRAK-50 requires 50 checkpoints (may need separate training run with checkpoint saving)
pilots.fallback: If TRAK-50 too slow: start with TRAK-10 as initial ground truth, compute TRAK-50 asynchronously

pilots.description: Run two-way ANOVA on 100 test points. Verify statistical pipeline, check for numerical issues, estimate effect sizes. This gives an early signal on H-G1 before full 500-point experiment.
pilots.pass_criteria: ANOVA runs without error AND residual fraction computable for all 3 metrics AND no metric yields NaN AND at least one metric shows residual > 15% (early signal for gate)
pilots.risk: Low - pure statistics on cached data
pilots.fallback: If ANOVA unstable with 100 points (10/class): use permutation test instead of parametric ANOVA

pilots.description: Compute BSS for 100 test points on seed 42 model. Verify eigendecomposition feasibility, bucket boundary selection, and BSS_outlier variance.
pilots.pass_criteria: GGN top-100 eigendecomposition completes without OOM AND outlier bucket (lambda>100) has >=5 eigenvalues AND BSS_outlier has non-trivial variance (std > 0.01) AND within-class BSS_outlier variance > 10% of total
pilots.risk: Medium - eigendecomposition may OOM or be very slow for full-model GGN
pilots.fallback: If full GGN eigendecomposition OOM: use Kronecker-factored approximation for eigendecomposition (EK-FAC already factored). If still OOM: reduce to top-50 eigenvectors.

pilots.description: Exact LOO on CIFAR-10/500 subset (500 training samples, 10 test points). Verify retraining pipeline and LOO-TRAK correlation on micro scale.
pilots.pass_criteria: LOO retraining completes for 10 test points x 500 removals within timeout AND LOO attribution rankings are non-degenerate (not all identical) AND LOO-TRAK correlation > 0.2 on this subset
pilots.risk: High - even 500x10=5000 retrainings may be slow
pilots.fallback: If too slow: (1) reduce to 200 training samples x 5 test points, (2) use proximal Bregman retraining (Basu et al.), (3) use approximate LOO via Newton step

pilot_execution_order.gate: All 4 attribution methods produce valid output
ANOVA pipeline works AND BSS computable AND LOO feasible at micro scale

critical_pilot_reason: If full-model Hessian computation is not feasible (OOM or API limitation), the entire experimental design must be revised to either use last-layer-only (with known hierarchy collapse risk) or custom implementation (significant engineering overhead).
